content: {"perspective": "Economic", "question": "What is the evidence base on the cost-effectiveness of intermittent fasting as an intervention for metabolic syndrome — specifically, have any published economic evaluations quantified the incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (ICER) per quality-adjusted life year (QALY) gained, budget impact, or long-run cost-offset from averted type-2 diabetes and cardiovascular events, compared with standard lifestyle counseling, structured Mediterranean-diet programs, or GLP-1 receptor agonists such as semaglutide and tirzepatide, and how do those values compare against conventional willingness-to-pay thresholds used by NICE, CADTH, and ICER?", "search_queries": ["intermittent fasting cost-effectiveness QALY ICER metabolic syndrome type 2 diabetes decision model", "time-restricted eating budget impact analysis prevention type 2 diabetes NICE cost-effectiveness", "semaglutide tirzepatide cost-effectiveness QALY lifestyle intervention comparison obesity metabolic", "lifestyle intervention cost-effectiveness diabetes prevention Markov model willingness to pay threshold", "fasting mimicking diet economic evaluation cost-effectiveness metabolic syndrome budget impact"]}
